subject: [PATCH 2/2] btrfs: prevent direct reclaim during compressed readahead

reviews.author: Qu Wenruo
reviews.summary:
Reviewer Qu Wenruo requested that the patch explanation provide a clear justification for why the existing GFP flags are problematic, as stated in the cover letter.

Reviewer Qu Wenruo expressed concerns that the patch may cause readahead to be skipped for large folios, potentially leading to re-reads and re-decompression, and suggested disabling compressed readahead altogether and relying on large folios instead.
reviews.raw_body:
I believe it will be more convincing to explain why the current gfp 
flags is going to cause the problem you mentioned in the cover letter.

---

After reading the code, I have a feeling that, we shouldn't act on the 
behalf of MM layer to add the next few folios into the page cache.

On the other hand, with the incoming large folios, we will completely 
skip the readahead for large folios.

I know this is not optimal as the next few folios may still belong to 
the same compressed extent and will cause re-read and re-decompression.

Thus I'm wondering, for your specific workload, will disabling 
compressed ra completely and fully rely on large folios help?

If the performance is acceptable, I'd prefer to disable compressed 
readahead completely and rely on large folios instead.

(Now I understand why other fses with compression support is completely 
relying on fixed IO size)

Thanks,
Qu

reviews.author: Mark Harmstone
reviews.summary: Reviewer Mark Harmstone expressed concerns about the patch being a quick fix for a production issue, suggesting it may not be the ultimate solution and proposing reworking the whole thing as a more stable approach.
reviews.raw_body:
Your idea might have merit, but this is a quick fix for a problem that 
JP has seen in production. Reworking the whole thing might be the 
ultimate solution, but that's much riskier, and will take more testing, 
than the proposed change.

reviews.author: JP (Meta) (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledges a concern about clarity in the patch description, agrees to add more information on why current flags are problematic, and promises to revise the cover letter text.
reviews.raw_body:
I see that now.

Qu also had a good point about including info on why the current flags
are an issue. Some of the cover letter text would help here. I'll bring
some of that over and expand on it so we have the relevant details here.

2026-03-20.patch_summary: This patch prevents direct reclaim during compressed readahead in Btrfs by passing specific GFP flags when allocating memory for readahead operations. The new gfp_t API is used to restrict direct reclaim, and a bool parameter is added to the add_ra_bio_pages function to allow callers to specify whether direct reclaim should be restricted or not. This change affects the btrfs_submit_compressed_read function and the add_ra_bio_pages function in fs/btrfs/compression.c.
